The Lekki shootings were a tragic incident where security forces opened fire on peaceful protesters in Lekki, Lagos, on November 20, 2020. The demonstrations were part of the #EndSARS movement, which sought to end the harassment and brutality of the Special Anti-Robbery Squad (SARS) in Nigeria. Many young people were killed or injured during the dispersal of the crowd, sparking widespread outrage both within Nigeria and internationally.

In the aftermath, the Nigerian government established a panel of inquiry to investigate the incident, though the findings and subsequent actions have been the subject of intense debate and criticism. The event highlighted deep-seated issues regarding police conduct, human rights, and the treatment of youth in the country. It also galvanized a global conversation about police brutality and the right to peaceful assembly.
